stimulus: In the early twenty-first century, scholars in literary studies devoted increasing attention to questions of reading practice. Proponents of computational approaches mapped reading patterns across thousands of digitized novels through natural-language processing pipelines; ______blank literary critic Rita Felski characterized reading as "a mode of attentive, embodied engagement with a text's affective pull," emphasizing instead the situated, lived experience of individual readers.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The sentence opposes two scholarly approaches—a computational, large-scale method and an experiential, individual-reader method. "In contrast" precisely signals this opposition between the two approaches.

Choice A is incorrect because "in addition" would mark Felski's approach as a parallel supplement to computational methods, but the passage emphasizes their difference. Choice B is incorrect because "for instance" would make Felski's view an example of computational practice, which it is not. Choice C is incorrect because "consequently" would signal cause and effect, but Felski's view is not a result of the rise of computational approaches.
